extraction.one_line_summary: CAVAGNA attacked solo 25km out on flat approach to Toledo, held off peloton by 26 seconds on technical cobbled uphill finish.
tactical_patterns: CAVAGNA's solo move worked because peloton slowed on crash-induced neutralization, then hesitated to chase aggressively on narrow technical roads.
BENNETT positioned perfectly on BAUHAUS wheel for bunch sprint but tactical approach failed; should have anticipated gradient forcing early launch.
Early-stage speed bump crash involving MARTIN devastated Jumbo-Visma's chase capacity, demonstrating critical vulnerability of relying on single domestique in final week.

rider_signals.CAVAGNA Rémi: positive: executed 25km solo attack on flat roads into technical finish, showing exceptional third-week stamina and commitment beyond typical breakaway rider
rider_signals.BENNETT Sam: neutral: won bunch sprint for second but failed stage win; positioning discipline excellent but lacked power to launch early on gradient
rider_signals.MARTIN Tony: negative: crashed on speed bumps mid-stage, eliminating ROGLIČ's primary chase domestique before crucial final stretch
rider_signals.LÓPEZ Miguel Ángel: positive: aggressive riding moved him within striking distance of podium at 4:17, showing maintained form in week three
rider_signals.VALVERDE Alejandro: neutral: gained only 3 seconds on ROGLIČ despite crash chaos, revealing limited ability to exploit GC rival's misfortune
rider_signals.ROGLIČ Primož: neutral: navigated crash and chaos without time loss, extending GC lead to 2:50 through risk management rather than aggression
